record.field:
#Variables cron_id = env.ref('cap_project_test.ir_cron_session_test') issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('ir_cron_id', '=', cron_id.id)]) quality_category = issue_type.quality_category today = datetime.date.today() seven_days_before = today + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=-7) role_ids = env['planning.role'].search(['|', ('is_configurator', '=', True), ('is_developer', '=', True)])
#Projects to consider : with status "Pre Live", not "on hold" and not "Test excluded" project_ids = env['project.project'].search([('exclude_for_tests', '=', False), ('project_status_id.code', '=', 'pre_live'), ('on_hold_reason', '=', False), ('create_date', '>', '2024-01-01')])
#Loop on filtered projects to check for project_id in project_ids: # Get the Employee from all the employees employee_id = issue_type.get_employee(project_id, quality_category.role_ids) #For Warning Type of Quality issue logs if quality_category.warning_before_penalty: #Get the last penalty log before create warning log last_penalty_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'penalty'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1)
# Create a warning only if: # - No penalty log exists yet, or # - The last penalty log is older than 4 days if not last_penalty_log or (last_penalty_log and last_penalty_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=4) <= today): first_day_of_the_month = today.replace(day=1) domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('logged_date', '>=', first_day_of_the_month)] msg = "WARNING: \nNot Enough Session Test Created for Project : {}, A minimum of 2 session test is required per 100 consumed hours. If the required session tests are not created, a Penalty quality issue will be raised.".format(project_id.name) type = 'warning'
#Timesheets to consider: timesheets logged for Developer and Configurator only timesheet_ids = project_id.timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.task_id.role_id.id in role_ids.ids) #Calculate consumed quantity on the project for tasks with role conf, dev only consumed_qty = int(sum(timesheet_ids.mapped('unit_amount')))
#We only consider the necessity of making tests if number of produced hours is higher than 100h if consumed_qty >= 100: session_test_id = env['session.test'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id)]) #If there is no test sessions or if there is less than 2 tests every 100 hours if not session_test_id or (2 * int(consumed_qty / 100)) > len(session_test_id): # Create a warning log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type)
#For Penalty Type of Quality issue logs #Get the last warning log before create penalty log last_warning_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'warning'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1)
# Create a penalty log if: # - Warnings are not required before penalties, or # - A warning exists and it is older than 3 days if not quality_category.warning_before_penalty or (last_warning_log and last_warning_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=3) <= today): domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('logged_date', '>=', seven_days_before)] msg = 'Not Enough Session Test Created for Project : {}'.format(project_id.name) type = 'penalty'
#Timesheets to consider: timesheets logged for Developer and Configurator only timesheet_ids = project_id.timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.task_id.role_id.id in role_ids.ids) #Calculate consumed quantity on the project for tasks with role conf, dev only consumed_qty = int(sum(timesheet_ids.mapped('unit_amount')))
#We only consider the necessity of making tests if number of produced hours is higher than 100h if consumed_qty >= 100: session_test_id = env['session.test'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('status', 'in', ['closed', 'signed'])])
#If there is no test sessions or if there is less than 2 tests every 100 hours if not session_test_id or (2 * int(consumed_qty / 100)) > len(session_test_id): # Create a warning log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type)

#Variables cron_id = env.ref('cap_project_test.ir_cron_task_test') issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('ir_cron_id', '=', cron_id.id)]) quality_category = issue_type.quality_category today = datetime.date.today() seven_days_before = today + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=-7) role_ids = env['planning.role'].search(['|', ('is_configurator', '=', True), ('is_developer', '=', True)])
#Projects to consider : with status "Pre Live", not "on hold" and not "Test excluded" project_ids = env['project.project'].search([('exclude_for_tests', '=', False), ('project_status_id.code', '=', 'pre_live'), ('on_hold_reason', '=', False), ('create_date', '>', '2024-01-01')])
#Loop on filtered projects to check for project_id in project_ids: # Get the Employee from all the employees employee_id = issue_type.get_employee(project_id, quality_category.role_ids)
#For Warning Type of Quality issue logs if quality_category.warning_before_penalty: #Get the last penalty log before create warning log last_penalty_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'penalty'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1)
# Create a warning only if: # - No penalty log exists yet, or # - The last penalty log is older than 4 days if not last_penalty_log or (last_penalty_log and last_penalty_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=4) <= today): first_day_of_the_month = today.replace(day=1) domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('logged_date', '>=', first_day_of_the_month)] msg = "WARNING: \nNot Enough Task Tests Created for Project : {}, A minimum of 10 Custom Task Tests are required per 100 consumed hours. If the required Task tests are not created, a Penalty quality issue will be raised.".format(project_id.name) type = 'warning'
#Timesheets to consider: timesheets logged for Developer and Configurator only timesheet_ids = project_id.timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.task_id.role_id.id in role_ids.ids) #Calculate consumed quantity on the project for tasks with role conf, dev only consumed_qty = int(sum(timesheet_ids.mapped('unit_amount')))
#We only consider the necessity of making tests if number of produced hours is higher than 100h if consumed_qty >= 100: #Count number of tests currently written on the project task_test_id = env['test.test'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('template_test_id', '=', False)]) # Required number of tests = 10 tests per 100 consumed hours if not task_test_id or (10 * int(consumed_qty / 100)) > len(task_test_id): # Create a warning log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type)
#For Penalty Type of Quality issue logs #Get the last warning log before create penalty log last_warning_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'warning'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1)
# Create a penalty log if: # - Warnings are not required before penalties, or # - A warning exists and it is older than 3 days if not quality_category.warning_before_penalty or (last_warning_log and last_warning_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=3) <= today): domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('logged_date', '>=', seven_days_before)] msg = 'Not Enough Task Tests Created for Project : {}'.format(project_id.name) type = 'penalty'
#Timesheets to consider: timesheets logged for Developer and Configurator only timesheet_ids = project_id.timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.task_id.role_id.id in role_ids.ids) #Calculate consumed quantity on the project for tasks with role conf, dev only consumed_qty = int(sum(timesheet_ids.mapped('unit_amount')))
#We only consider the necessity of making tests if number of produced hours is higher than 100h if consumed_qty >= 100: #Count number of tests currently written on the project task_test_id = env['test.test'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('template_test_id', '=', False)]) # Required number of tests = 10 tests per 100 consumed hours if not task_test_id or (10 * int(consumed_qty / 100)) > len(task_test_id): # Create a penalty log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type)

#variables cron_id = env.ref('cap_project_test.ir_cron_feedback_not_handled') issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('ir_cron_id', '=', cron_id.id)]) quality_category = issue_type.quality_category today = datetime.date.today() seven_days_before = today + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=-7)
#Projects to consider : Not "feedback excluded", In "prelive" status, not "on hold" project_ids = env['project.project'].search([('project_status_id.code', '=', 'pre_live'), ('on_hold_reason', '=', False), ('exclude_for_feedback', '=', False)])
#Loop on projects to check for project_id in project_ids: #count number of feedbacks with a status in "new" for more than 7 days project_feedback_ids = model.search([('create_date', '<=', seven_days_before), ('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('status', '=','new')]) #If one or more feedback items remain unresolved, a quality issue will be raised. if len(project_feedback_ids) >= 1: # Get the Employee from all the employees employee_id = issue_type.get_employee(project_id, quality_category.role_ids)
#For Warning Type of Quality issue logs if quality_category.warning_before_penalty: #Get the last penalty log before create warning log last_penalty_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'penalty'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1) # Create a warning only if: # - No penalty log exists yet, or # - The last penalty log is older than 4 days if not last_penalty_log or (last_penalty_log and last_penalty_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=4) <= today): first_day_of_the_month = today.replace(day=1) domain = [('logged_date', '>=', first_day_of_the_month), ('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('create_date', '>', '2024-01-01')] msg = "WARNING: \nFor project: {}, one or more feedback items have not yet been addressed. If these feedback items remain unhandled, a Penalty quality issue will be raised.".format(project_id.name) type = 'warning' # Create a warning log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type, False, False)
#For Penalty Type of Quality issue logs #Get the last warning log before create penalty log last_warning_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'warning'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1) # Create a penalty log if: # - Warnings are not required before penalties, or # - A warning exists and it is older than 3 days if not quality_category.warning_before_penalty or (last_warning_log and last_warning_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=3) <= today): domain = [('logged_date', '>=', seven_days_before), ('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('create_date', '>', '2024-01-01')] msg = "For project: {}, one or more feedback items are still pending and have not been handled.".format(project_id.name) type = 'penalty' # Create a penalty log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type, False, False)

#Variables cron_id = env.ref('cap_project_test.ir_cron_not_enough_feedback') issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('ir_cron_id', '=', cron_id.id)]) quality_category = issue_type.quality_category today = datetime.date.today() seven_days_before = today + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=-7) role_ids = env['planning.role'].search(['|', ('is_configurator', '=', True), ('is_developer', '=', True)])
#Projects to consider : Not "feedback excluded", In "prelive" status, not "on hold" project_ids = env['project.project'].search([('exclude_for_feedback', '=', False), ('project_status_id.code', '=', 'pre_live'), ('on_hold_reason', '=', False), ('create_date', '>', '2024-01-01')])
#Loop on projects to check for project_id in project_ids: # Get the Employee from all the employees employee_id = issue_type.get_employee(project_id, quality_category.role_ids) #For Warning Type of Quality issue logs if quality_category.warning_before_penalty: #Get the last penalty log before create warning log last_penalty_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'penalty'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1)
# Create a warning only if: # - No penalty log exists yet, or # - The last penalty log is older than 4 days if not last_penalty_log or (last_penalty_log and last_penalty_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=4) <= today): first_day_of_the_month = today.replace(day=1) domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('logged_date', '>=', first_day_of_the_month)] msg = "WARNING: \nNot Enough Feedback Created for Project : {}, A minimum of 1 Feedback is required per 100 consumed hours. If the required Feedbacks are not created, a Penalty quality issue will be raised.".format(project_id.name) type = 'warning'
#Timesheets to consider: timesheets logged for Developer and Configurator only timesheet_ids = project_id.timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.task_id.role_id.id in role_ids.ids) #Calculate consumed quantity on the project for tasks with role conf, dev only consumed_qty = int(sum(timesheet_ids.mapped('unit_amount')))
# Required number of Feedbacks = 1 Feedback per 100 consumed hours if consumed_qty >= 100: #count number of feedbacks on the project feedback_ids = env['project.feedback'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id)]) if not feedback_ids or int(consumed_qty / 100) > len(feedback_ids): # Create a warning log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type)
#For Penalty Type of Quality issue logs #Get the last warning log before create penalty log last_warning_log = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('employee_id', '=', employee_id.id), ('log_type', '=', 'warning'), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)], order='id desc', limit=1)
# Create a penalty log if: # - Warnings are not required before penalties, or # - A warning exists and it is older than 3 days if not quality_category.warning_before_penalty or (last_warning_log and last_warning_log.logged_date + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=3) <= today): domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id), ('logged_date', '>=', seven_days_before)] msg = 'Not Enough Feedback Created for Project : {}'.format(project_id.name) type = 'penalty'
#Timesheets to consider: timesheets logged for Developer and Configurator only timesheet_ids = project_id.timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.task_id.role_id.id in role_ids.ids) #Calculate consumed quantity on the project for tasks with role conf, dev only consumed_qty = int(sum(timesheet_ids.mapped('unit_amount')))
# Required number of tests = 10 Feedbacks per 100 consumed hours if consumed_qty >= 100: #count number of feedbacks on the project feedback_ids = env['project.feedback'].search([('project_id', '=', project_id.id)]) if not feedback_ids or int(consumed_qty / 100) > len(feedback_ids): # Create a penalty log for each role concerned by this quality issue for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: issue_type.check_planning_role(project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type)
